articles.title: BITCOIN RISES AS OIL SPIKES, EQUITIES DROP
articles.summary: Bitcoin rose 2.8% during European trading hours even as US equity futures fell more than 1.5% and oil increased to $115 per barrel amid the Iran conflict and supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts noted that while BTC has yet to fully earn its digital gold narrative, its practical use case as a digital escape hatch is becoming increasingly relevant, particularly in Gulf countries, amid episodes of currency volatility and political uncertainty, highlighting Bitcoin's resilience to oil shocks and geopolitical risks.

articles.title: STRATEGY BUYS $1.3B IN BITCOIN, HOLDINGS HIT 738,731 BTC
articles.summary: Strategy purchased 17,994 BTC for $1.28 billion at an average price of $70,946, bringing total holdings to 738,731 BTC worth roughly $50 billion and representing over 3.4% of Bitcoin's fixed supply. The buys were funded through ongoing at-the-market equity and preferred stock programs as part of its broader $84 billion capital plan, even as the firm sits on approximately $6 billion in unrealized losses and its stock trades near net asset value. Michael Saylor dubbed the milestone “the second century,” marking over 100 separate Bitcoin acquisitions as corporate treasury competition intensifies.

articles.title: NASDAQ AND KRAKEN BUILD AN EQUITIES-TO-DEFI GATEWAY
articles.summary: Nasdaq is partnering with Kraken parent Payward to build an "equities transformation gateway" that uses Kraken's xStocks framework to let tokenized equities move between regulated markets and decentralized blockchain networks while keeping issuer control, investor protections, and legal shareholder rights anchored on Nasdaq's permissioned infrastructure. The gateway aims to create interoperable onchain liquidity for tokenized securities, with Nasdaq's equity token design and related services expected to begin rolling out in the first half of 2027.

articles.title: POLYGON INCREASES TO 78% OF X402 AGENT PAYMENT FACILITATORS
articles.summary: New Dune data shows x402 agent payments consolidating around Solana, Base, and Polygon, with Polygon recently capturing over 60% of facilitators and peaking at 78% in a single day. The spike follows Polygon's Lisovo upgrade, $1M in gas subsidies for agents, and the launch of a new Agent CLI, signaling an aggressive push to position Polygon as the default settlement layer for AI-driven payments.

articles.title: LAST WEEK IN SOLANA PAYMENTS
articles.summary: Solana stablecoin transaction volume reached a record $650B in February, doubling the previous all-time high. TradFi and emerging market integrations deepened as Crossmint backed Western Union's USDPT stablecoin launch with wallet and payment API infrastructure, while Raenest's 1M+ African fintech user base gained USDT/USDC support on Solana, and the Noah-Nafolo partnership targeted remittance corridors to cut up to 12% in legacy bank fees. Crossmint also previewed agentic payment cards enabling AI agents to transact via Visa Intelligent Commerce.

articles.title: POLYGON: STABLECOINS ACTUALLY BECOMING PAYMENTS ON POLYGON
articles.summary: Raw stablecoin transfer volume on Polygon spiked to ~$140B in May 2025 but, after adjusting for automated flows, “real” monthly transfers stayed near $5 to 10B year-round, while app-level adjusted volume grew steadily from ~ $500M/month to more than $2B in Jan 2026 driven by Avenia, Rio, Revolut, BlindPay and the late-year rise of Tazapay. Average raw transaction size briefly increased to ~$1,800 in April to May before collapsing under $200 by year-end (adjusted averages fell more smoothly from ~$750 to ~$550), C2B payments overtook C2C (C2B was greater than $200M/month by year-end), and circulating stablecoin supply on Polygon roughly doubled from $1.6B to $3B.

articles.title: VITALIK ON BUILDING DEMOCRATIC TECH IN A “CHAOTIC ERA”
articles.summary: Enthusiasm for DAOs, quadratic funding, onchain voting, and broader democratic tech has faded, especially as we see a broader “authoritarian wave” driven by disillusionment with democratic systems. Today's chaotic era leaves little room for ambitious mechanism design compared to the more stable 2000s and 2010s. Instead of pushing rigid, binding governance systems, focus on consensus-finding tools like anonymous polling, assurance contracts, and Pol.is style coordination that surface widely supported proposals and give distributed groups a credible collective voice. In moments of instability, democratic tech should function less as a replacement order and more as “sanctuary tools” that help communities signal legitimacy and influence real power in real time.

articles.title: DOLLAR STABLES ARE BLEEDING VALUE; IT'S TIME TO DIVERSIFY
articles.summary: On-chain charts show nearly all stablecoin supply is USD-denominated, so non-US holders have lost roughly 10% purchasing power as the dollar fell versus the euro since January 2025. Crypto users can hedge by diversifying into non-USD stables like EURC (~€393M circulation, MiCA-compliant, yields ~2.6% on Aave/ETH and ~2.9% on Fluid/Base), EURcv (Societe Generale-FORGE, institutional focus), and VCHF (onchain Swiss franc under Liechtenstein rules).

articles.title: CIRCLE AND STRIPE RACING TO BUILD AI AGENT STABLECOIN PAYMENTS
articles.summary: Circle Internet Group and Stripe are both building payment infrastructure targeting autonomous AI agents that settle transactions in stablecoins, positioning for a potential shift away from card-based payments.

articles.title: USDC FLIPS USDT IN TRANSACTION VOLUME
articles.summary: USDC has consistently flipped USDT in transaction volume over recent months, with USDC volumes reaching new ATHs at more than 2x USDT's – notable given Tether is valued roughly 20x Circle's current market cap.

articles.title: CURVE FINANCE ACCUSES PANCAKESWAP OF CODE COPYING
articles.summary: Curve Finance publicly accused PancakeSwap of illegally copying its stableswap code following the launch of PancakeSwap Infinity's StableSwap feature.

articles.title: PUDGY PENGUINS FACES TRADEMARK LAWSUIT FROM ORIGINAL PENGUIN
articles.summary: PEI Licensing, parent company of the Original Penguin apparel brand, has filed a trademark infringement lawsuit against Pudgy Penguins, alleging the NFT project's merchandise marks deceive consumers – a claim rooted in PEI's penguin trademark dating back to 1956.
